text: In this bright, modern corridor, I feel a sense of anticipation. The glass-walled room awaits with neatly arranged chairs, inviting yet sterile. Everything is angled, almost vertiginous—like the world is tilted off its axis. I am reminded of how spaces can both welcome and estrange us. I wonder what kind of gathering will convene here—what ideas, what words, what silences might fill this emptiness. Each detail, from the fire hose to the illuminated exit, makes me aware of the constructed nature of safety and departure.
